name: LUCENT LUNG LESIONS
definition: reduction in pulmonary blood volume tissue necrosis with bronchial drainage round circumscribed space surrounded by an epithelial / fibrous wall of uniform /
dominant_finding: hyperlucent lung on side which patient rotates toward tube / away from film ←
distribution: very thin-walled cavities less likely to follow the apical distribution of TB /
discriminating_features: hyperlucent lung on side which patient rotates toward tube / away from film ←

general: hyperlucent lung on side which patient rotates toward tube / away from film ←
level of inflation beyond 8th rib posteriorly
depressed configuration of hemidiaphragms best judged on LAT view

multiple thin-walled lucencies with air-fluid levels in lower lobes
often seen at end of feeding vessel
lobular architecture preserved with bronchovascular bundle in central position,
cysts with walls arrayed in a single subpleural tier
randomly scattered cysts surrounded by normal lung
3-10-mm small irregular thick-walled cystic air spaces usually of comparable
cysts with walls of variable thickness + irregular shape
predilection for upper lung zones with relative sparing of lung bases
thickening of interlobular septa + bronchovascular bundles

clinical.Rx: surgical resection if bulla > ⅓ of hemithorax
